title: O2 Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1 Sensor 1
system: O2 Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1 Sensor 1
possible_causes: O2 Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1 Sensor 1 signal range or performance fault.
O2 sensor, heater influence, exhaust leak, wiring, connector, power/ground, fuel control, air/fuel mixture, catalyst, or related DTC influence.
Bank/sensor location, reference values, and test conditions vary by vehicle and must be confirmed in the manufacturer service manual.
check_order: Save DTCs, freeze-frame data, fuel trims, O2/AF sensor data, catalyst monitor data, exhaust leak findings, and related DTCs.
Confirm vehicle-specific bank and sensor location, connector terminals, reference values, warm-up state, fuel-control conditions, and test method in the manufacturer service manual.
Inspect wiring, connectors, exhaust leaks, intake leaks, fuel trim abnormalities, sensor heater operation, and sensor response under specified conditions.
Separate sensor, circuit, exhaust, fuel-control, and catalyst causes using the manufacturer service manual procedure.
common_mistakes: Treating bank/sensor numbering or normal voltage/current ranges as vehicle-universal.
Replacing the sensor before checking exhaust leaks, wiring, fuel trim, and heater operation.
Testing sensor response without the specified warm-up and operating conditions.
safety_notes: Use strong caution around hot exhaust components, rotating parts, and fuel-system work; secure the vehicle, avoid burns, and follow the manufacturer procedure.
source: SAE J2012 / SAE J2012DA generic DTC definitions and Autel official DTC table based diagnostic guidance

title: O2 Sensor Negative Voltage Bank 1 Sensor 1
system: O2 Sensor Negative Voltage Bank 1 Sensor 1
possible_causes: O2 Sensor Negative Voltage Bank 1 Sensor 1 signal fault or negative voltage condition.
O2 sensor signal circuit, bias/reference circuit, wiring, connector, sensor internal fault, exhaust condition, power/ground, or related DTC influence.
Vehicle-specific sensor type, bias voltage, and diagnostic thresholds must be confirmed in the manufacturer service manual.
check_order: Save DTCs, freeze-frame data, O2/AF sensor data, fuel trims, heater data, and related DTCs.
Confirm vehicle-specific sensor type, bank/sensor location, connector terminals, bias/reference values, measurement conditions, and pinpoint test steps in the manufacturer service manual.
Inspect signal/reference circuits, wiring, connectors, heater operation, exhaust leaks, and sensor response under specified conditions.
Separate sensor, circuit, exhaust, and fuel-control causes using the manufacturer procedure.
common_mistakes: Assuming all O2 and air-fuel ratio sensors use the same voltage behavior.
Condemning the sensor without checking reference/bias circuit and wiring faults.
Ignoring exhaust leaks or mixture faults that can affect sensor behavior.
safety_notes: Use strong caution around hot exhaust components, rotating parts, and fuel-system work; secure the vehicle, avoid burns, and follow the manufacturer procedure.
source: SAE J2012 / SAE J2012DA generic DTC definitions and Autel official DTC table based diagnostic guidance

title: NOx Exceedence - Empty Reagent Tank
system: NOx Exceedence - Empty Reagent Tank
possible_causes: NOx Exceedence - Empty Reagent Tank aftertreatment NOx exceedance or reagent/EGR control fault.
DEF/reagent level or quality, dosing interruption, reagent consumption, EGR flow, SCR/NOx sensor system, wiring, connector, reductant system, exhaust leak, or related DTC influence.
Root cause and inducement strategy vary by vehicle and require manufacturer service information.
check_order: Save DTCs, freeze-frame data, NOx sensor data, reagent level/quality data, dosing command, SCR temperature, EGR command/actual data, and related DTCs.
Confirm vehicle-specific reagent specification, service test conditions, NOx sensor locations, dosing system checks, EGR flow tests, and inducement/reset procedure in the manufacturer service manual.
Inspect reagent quantity and quality, dosing interruption causes, leaks, wiring/connectors, NOx sensor data plausibility, exhaust leaks, and EGR operation.
Separate reagent, dosing, NOx sensor, SCR catalyst, EGR, exhaust, and control-module causes using the manufacturer procedure.
common_mistakes: Adding non-specified reagent or assuming reagent quality without test confirmation.
Clearing inducement-related codes without repairing the root cause and following service procedure.
Treating NOx sensor or EGR thresholds as vehicle-universal.
safety_notes: Use strong caution around hot exhaust/aftertreatment components, reagent handling, and possible vehicle derate or inducement procedures; follow the manufacturer safety and service procedure.
source: SAE J2012 / SAE J2012DA generic DTC definitions and Autel official DTC table based diagnostic guidance
